FARC dissidents.description: Groups of former FARC members who refused to demobilize after the 2016 Colombian peace process or resumed insurgency. Numbering 2,000-2,500 armed combatants, they are heavily involved in cocaine production and sales. They operate in rural Colombia and Venezuela, controlling areas as a parallel state. Led by former mid-level commanders, they are a major security challenge for Colombia.

descriptions: FARC dissidents and Clan del Golfo are in direct armed conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 2020, it was revealed that the presence of FARC dissidents in northern Antioquia instigated a direct armed conflict with the Clan del Golfo known as Operation Mil.

Green Gang.description: The Green Gang was a Chinese secret society and criminal triad organization prominent in Shanghai during the early to mid 20th century. It originated from the Luojiao Buddhist sect and grain transport boatmen along the Grand Canal. The gang engaged in criminal activities such as opium trafficking and operated through networks of hometown associations. Key leaders included Du Yuesheng and Huang Jinrong.

descriptions: The Green Gang absorbed or eliminated the Axe Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: With the consolidation of Nationalist control and the strengthening of larger syndicates such as the Green Gang, smaller independent militant groups like the Axe Gang were gradually eliminated or absorbed.

descriptions: Green Gang financially supported the Kuomintang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Green Gang was a major financial supporter of Chiang Kai-shek

descriptions: Green Gang is a splinter of the Hung Society.
edges.evidence_quote: Notable organisations included the Green Gang, another Hung Society splinter

descriptions: Infobox lists Green gang as an ally of Hopi Boys.

edges.evidence_quote: In Watts , the Grape Street Crips and the PJ Watts Crips have feuded so much that the PJ Watts Crips even teamed up with a local Blood set, the Bounty Hunter Bloods , to fight the Grape Street Crips.

descriptions: PJ Watts Crips teamed up with Bounty Hunter Bloods against Grape Street Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: the PJ Watts Crips even teamed up with a local Blood set, the Bounty Hunter Bloods , to fight the Grape Street Crips.

descriptions: Club Deroes, Coffin Cheaters, Gypsy Jokers, and Gods Garbage cooperated to eject the Mongrel Mob from Perth in 1989.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 these four gangs combined forces to violently eject the New Zealand-based street gang, the Mongrel Mob which was attempting to establish itself in Perth.

descriptions: The Gianola brothers and Vitale gang fought a war for control of Detroit's Italian underworld.
edges.evidence_quote: In late 1918, Vitale broke away from the Gianola gang and established himself independently, aligning himself with the remnants of the Bosco group. What became known as the 'Gianola-Vitale War' emerged as one of the bloodiest battles in the history of the Detroit Mafia.

descriptions: M-19 was inspired by Montoneros.
edges.evidence_quote: It was inspired by other South American urban guerrilla groups, such as the Tupamaros in Uruguay and the Montoneros in Argentina.

edges.evidence_quote: Dung Hà was acquainted with and loved Hùng 'the warbler', who was a reputable gang boss in the areas around the bus station

descriptions: Bandidos absorbed Destroyers chapters by patching them over.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos expanded into Germany in June 2000 when the club "patched over" seventeen chapters of the Ghostrider's, Road Eagles and Destroyers that had been serving as probationary chapters since the previous year.

edges.evidence_quote: The Surčin clan is a component of the Serbian mafia.

descriptions: The Zemun Clan attempted to assassinate the leader of the Surčin clan, Ljubiša Buha, twice in 2002.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2002, the clan attempted to assassinate Buha twice: once in front of his company Defense Road in Belgrade, and again during his vacation in Istanbul.

descriptions: The Zemun Clan initially operated under the control or in cooperation with the Surčin clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Presumably, the group operated either under the control, or in cooperation with, the Surčin clan, the largest organised car theft group in the country.

Cartel of the Suns.description: The Cartel de los Soles is a term used to describe criminal networks of drug trafficking and corruption within the Bolivarian National Armed Forces of Venezuela, allegedly emerging in the early 1990s. It involves high-ranking military and political officials, including Nicolás Maduro and Diosdado Cabello, accused by the United States of leading the network. The organization is not a traditional cartel but a series of disconnected cells within the Venezuelan military. The Venezuelan government denies the allegations, and some experts question its existence.

descriptions: Marriage between Clíver Alcalá (member of Cártel de los Soles) and Marta González (niece of Cartel de La Guajira capo) signified an alliance between the two cartels.
edges.evidence_quote: Según el medio colombiano Caracol Radio, el matrimonio entre Alcalá y González habría significado una alianza entre los carteles de La Guajira y el de Los Soles.

descriptions: Cartel de La Guajira conducted direct drug trafficking business with the Cártel de los Soles.
edges.evidence_quote: El cartel guajiro también realizó negocios directos relacionados al narcotráfico con el vicepresidente venezolano Tareck El Aissami, el Cartel de los Soles y Walid Makled.

Santa Cruz Cartel.description: Bolivian drug cartel headquartered in Santa Cruz de la Sierra, one of the largest in the country. Active since the 1980s, it has been involved in narco-terrorist incidents including assassination attempts on DEA agents and bombing attempts on politicians. The cartel is allied with Colombian, Brazilian, and Mexican drug trafficking organizations and engages in turf wars with smaller gangs.

Goda-ikka.description: The Goda-ikka is a yakuza group based in Yamaguchi, Japan, founded in 1948 as the Goda-gumi by Koichi Goda. It is the largest designated yakuza group in Yamaguchi Prefecture, with an estimated 30 members. The group has been a member of the anti-Yamaguchi federation Gosha-kai since 1996.

descriptions: Goda-ikka and Asano-gumi are both members of the Gosha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Goda-ikka has been a member of an anti- Yamaguchi federation, the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Kyodo-kai , the Asano-gumi , and the Shikoku -based Shinwa-kai .

descriptions: Goda-ikka is a member of the Gosha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Goda-ikka has been a member of an anti- Yamaguchi federation, the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Kyodo-kai , the Asano-gumi , and the Shikoku -based Shinwa-kai .

descriptions: Goda-ikka's founder was a member of Kagotora-gumi before forming his own group.
edges.evidence_quote: The group was formed in 1948 as the Goda-gumi ( 合田組 , Gōda-gumi ) in Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi by Koichi Goda, then a member of an old yakuza clan, the Kagotora-gumi.

descriptions: Goda-ikka and Kyodo-kai are both members of the Gosha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Goda-ikka has been a member of an anti- Yamaguchi federation, the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Kyodo-kai , the Asano-gumi , and the Shikoku -based Shinwa-kai .

descriptions: Goda-ikka and Kyosei-kai are both members of the Gosha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Goda-ikka has been a member of an anti- Yamaguchi federation, the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Kyodo-kai , the Asano-gumi , and the Shikoku -based Shinwa-kai .

descriptions: Goda-ikka and Shinwa-kai are both members of the Gosha-kai federation.

The Council.description: African-American organized crime syndicate in New York City that controlled the heroin trade in Harlem during the 1970s. Formed by Nicky Barnes in 1972, the seven-man organization handled local criminal disputes and drug distribution. Connected to the Italian-American Mafia, it was dissolved in 1983 after Barnes became a federal informant.

descriptions: The infobox lists The Council as an ally of the Black Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Council , Nation of Islam , Philadelphia crime family

descriptions: Black Mafia listed The Council as allies in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Council, Nation of Islam, Philadelphia crime family

Musitano crime family.description: The Musitano 'ndrina is a clan of the 'Ndrangheta criminal organization originating from Platì, Italy. It is active in northern Italy (Buccinasco and Bareggio), Canada (Hamilton, Ontario), and Brazil (Rio de Janeiro). The clan has been involved in drug trafficking, illegal waste disposal, and murder, including the 1997 killings of Johnny Papalia and Carmen Barillaro. Several members have been convicted, and leaders Angelo and Pat Musitano were killed in 2017 and 2020 respectively.

descriptions: The Musitano were historically part of the Barbaro group.
edges.evidence_quote: Ai tempi i Barbaro comprendevano anche le 'ndrine dei Perre , Trimboli , Agresta , Catanzariti , Sergi , Papalia , Molluso e Musitano .

descriptions: The Strangio are suspected of involvement in ship sinkings together with the Musitano.
edges.evidence_quote: I Nirta-Strangio sono sospettati di aver avuto a che fare con gli affondamenti di navi ... insieme ai Musitano di Platì

descriptions: Musitano crew rebelled against Buffalo family and murdered its Canadian faction boss Johnny Papalia.
edges.evidence_quote: Murdock told authorities that he was ordered by brothers Pasquale "Pat" Musitano and Angelo Musitano of the Musitano crew to murder both Papalia and Barillaro. According to Murdock, the Musitano brothers had been fed up with being a satellite (crew) of the Buffalo family and having to pay tribute money to the family.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Musitano crime family

descriptions: The Buffalo crime family is involved in a mob war with the Musitano crime family, exerting influence over the conflict.
